Peach Twig Borer.name: Rimon
Peach Twig Borer.stage: dormant, pink, petal fall, husk fall, summer
Peach Twig Borer.description: Use high rates for heavy populations. Spray interval: 10-14 days. Do not apply more than 3 times/season.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Harvanta 50SL
Peach Twig Borer.description: Toxic to bees. Do not make more than 3 applications per crop year. Rotate with other insecticide groups.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Imidan 70% WP
Peach Twig Borer.description: Toxic to bees. Do not apply more than 1 application per season.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Decis
Peach Twig Borer.description: Harmful to beneficial insects and mites. Residual control: 10 days.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Intrepid
Peach Twig Borer.description: Do not apply more than 2 times per season.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Altacor
Peach Twig Borer.description: Use pheromone traps to monitor. Do not exceed 261 g/ac per season.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Exirel
Peach Twig Borer.description: Toxic to bees. Do not apply more than 4 times/season. Use pheromone traps.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Altacor MaX
Peach Twig Borer.description: Do not exceed 131 g/ac per season.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Entrust
Peach Twig Borer.description: For suppression only. Do not apply more than 3 times per season.

Peach Twig Borer.name: Vayego 200 SC
Peach Twig Borer.description: Max 3 applications per year. Toxic to bees. Post-bloom only.

Peach Leaf Curl.name: Parasol
Peach Leaf Curl.description: Use higher rate for heavy rainfall and high disease pressure.

Peach Leaf Curl.name: Guardsman
Peach Leaf Curl.description: Apply at 75-100% leaf drop during dry weather.

Peach Leaf Curl.name: Copper Spray
Peach Leaf Curl.description: Apply at 75-100% leaf drop during dry weather. A spring dormant spray may also be required.

Peach Leaf Curl.name: Lime Sulphur 30%
Peach Leaf Curl.description: Apply early, before oil sprays and before bud swell.

Peach Leaf Curl.name: Bravo ZNC
Peach Leaf Curl.description: Do not apply after shuck split. Use higher rate for trees over 6 m in height.

Peach Leaf Curl.name: Echo
Peach Leaf Curl.description: Make one application per year at dormant spray or early spring.

European Red Mite.name: Purespray Green Oil
European Red Mite.description: Apply 20 L per 1000 L water at dormant stage; 4 L/ac in summer. Apply dormant to pre-bloom.

European Red Mite.name: Superior 70
European Red Mite.description: Do not use after green tip stage.

European Red Mite.name: Apollo
European Red Mite.description: Do not apply more than once/season. Residual: 45 days.

European Red Mite.name: Acramite
European Red Mite.description: Do not apply more than once/season. Residual: 21 days.

European Red Mite.name: Nealta
European Red Mite.description: Do not apply more than 2 times/season.

European Red Mite.name: Envidor
European Red Mite.description: Do not apply more than once per season.

San Jose Scale.name: Superior 70
San Jose Scale.description: Do not use after green tip stage.

McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.name: Apollo
McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.description: Do not apply more than once/season. Residual: 45 days.

McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.name: Acramite
McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.description: Do not apply more than once/season. Residual: 21 days.

McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.name: Nealta
McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.description: Do not apply more than 2 times/season.

McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.name: Envidor
McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.description: Do not apply more than once per season.

McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.name: Suffoil-X
McDaniel, Twospotted Spider Mites.description: Apply every 10-14 days. Do not apply more than 8/year.

Brown Rot.name: Quash
Brown Rot.description: Maximum 1 application/season.

Brown Rot.name: Cevya
Brown Rot.description: Do not make more than 2 sequential applications before alternating.

Brown Rot.name: Sercadis
Brown Rot.description: Non-ionic surfactant recommended.

Brown Rot.name: Merivon
Brown Rot.description: Limit Group 11 fungicides to 2 sprays/season.

Brown Rot.name: Luna Sensation
Brown Rot.description: Limit Group 11 fungicides to 2 applications per season.

Brown Rot.name: ProBlad
Brown Rot.description: Biological fungicide.

Brown Rot.name: Senator 50SC
Brown Rot.description: Maximum 2 applications/season.

Brown Rot.name: Elevate
Brown Rot.description: Avoid more than 2 consecutive applications.

Brown Rot.name: Funginex
Brown Rot.description: Do not apply after blossom.

Brown Rot.name: Nova 40WP
Brown Rot.description: Limit Group 3 fungicides to 2 sprays/season. Rotate with fungicides from other groups.

Brown Rot.name: Princeton
Brown Rot.description: Limit sprays of Group 3 fungicides to 2 per season.

Brown Rot.name: Serenade Opti
Brown Rot.description: Suppression only. Begin at early bloom.

Brown Rot.name: Fontelis
Brown Rot.description: Do not apply more than 2 times per season.

Brown Rot.name: Cantus
Brown Rot.description: Avoid more than 2 consecutive applications.

Brown Rot.name: Kenja
Brown Rot.description: Suppression only. Maximum of 3 applications per year.

Brown Rot.name: Pristine WG
Brown Rot.description: Also suppresses powdery mildew. Limit Group 11 fungicides to 2/season.

Brown Rot.name: Miravis Duo
Brown Rot.description: Max 2 applications per year.

Brown Rot.name: Captan L
Brown Rot.description: Use under low to moderate disease pressure.

Brown Rot.name: Maestro
Brown Rot.description: Do not apply with or near oil sprays.

Brown Rot.name: Bravo ZNC
Brown Rot.description: Do not apply after shuck split. Use higher rate for trees over 6 m.

Brown Rot.name: Echo
Brown Rot.description: Do not apply within 10 days of oil application.

Fruittree and European Leafrollers.name: Bioprotec CAF
Fruittree and European Leafrollers.description: Spray interval: 7 days under optimal conditions.

Fruittree and European Leafrollers.name: Exirel
Fruittree and European Leafrollers.description: Toxic to bees. Do not apply more than 4 times/season.

Fruittree and European Leafrollers.name: Success
Fruittree and European Leafrollers.description: Do not apply more than 3 times/season.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Bioprotec CAF
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Helps control exposed peach twig borer larvae.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Dipel 2X DF
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Helps control exposed peach twig borer larvae.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Rimon
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Do not apply more than 3 times/season.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Altacor
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Do not apply more than 3 times/season or 2 times above 87 g/ac.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Exirel
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Toxic to bees. Do not apply more than 4 times/season.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Harvanta 50SL
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Toxic to bees. Do not make more than 3 applications per crop year.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Altacor MaX
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Do not exceed 131 g/ac per season.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Delegate
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Do not apply more than 3 times a season.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Entrust
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Do not apply more than 3 times per season.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Success
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Do not apply more than 3 times/season.

Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.name: Vayego 200 SC
Obliquebanded and Threelined Leafrollers.description: Max 3 applications per year. Toxic to bees.

Eyespotted Bud Moth.name: Exirel
Eyespotted Bud Moth.description: Toxic to bees. Do not apply more than 4 times/season.

Eyespotted Bud Moth.name: Entrust
Eyespotted Bud Moth.description: Do not apply more than 3 times per season.

Eyespotted Bud Moth.name: Success
Eyespotted Bud Moth.description: Do not apply more than 3 times/season.

Western Flower Thrips.name: Harvanta 50SL
Western Flower Thrips.description: Toxic to bees. For suppression only. Do not make more than 3 applications.

Western Flower Thrips.name: Success
Western Flower Thrips.description: For nectarines only. Suppression only. Do not apply when bees forage.

Western Flower Thrips.name: Entrust
Western Flower Thrips.description: For nectarines only. Suppression only. Do not apply when bees forage.

Powdery Mildew.name: Miravis Duo
Powdery Mildew.description: Max 2 applications per year, then switch to non-Group 7 and 3.

Powdery Mildew.name: Flint Extra Fungicide
Powdery Mildew.description: Suppression only. Limit Group 11 fungicides to 2/season.

Powdery Mildew.name: Nova 40WP
Powdery Mildew.description: Limit Group 3 fungicides to 2 sprays/season. Rotate with fungicides from other groups.

Powdery Mildew.name: Cevya
Powdery Mildew.description: Do not make more than 2 sequential applications before alternating.

Powdery Mildew.name: Fontelis
Powdery Mildew.description: Do not apply more than 2 times per season.

Powdery Mildew.name: Luna Sensation
Powdery Mildew.description: Limit Group 11 fungicides to 2 applications per season.

Powdery Mildew.name: Kumulus DF
Powdery Mildew.description: May cause injury in hot weather. Do not apply more than 8 times/season.

Powdery Mildew.name: Purespray Green Oil
Powdery Mildew.description: For suppression. Apply in minimum 1000 L water/ha every 10-14 days.

Powdery Mildew.name: MilStop
Powdery Mildew.description: Suppression only. Do not mix with other pesticides.

Mealybugs.name: Movento 240 SC
Mealybugs.description: Apply with adjuvant. Toxic to bees.

Green Peach Aphid and Black Peach Aphid.name: Exirel
Green Peach Aphid and Black Peach Aphid.description: Do not apply more than 4 times/season. Toxic to bees.

Green Peach Aphid and Black Peach Aphid.name: Vayego 200 SC
Green Peach Aphid and Black Peach Aphid.description: Suppression only. Max 3 applications per year. Toxic to bees.

Green Peach Aphid and Black Peach Aphid.name: Closer
Green Peach Aphid and Black Peach Aphid.description: Do not apply more than 2 times/season.

Green Peach Aphid and Black Peach Aphid.name: Sivanto Prime
Green Peach Aphid and Black Peach Aphid.description: Do not apply more than 2000 mL/ha per season.

Peach Tree Borer.name: Isomate-P
Peach Tree Borer.description: Mating disruption pheromone. Apply before moth emergence.

Peach Tree Borer.name: Rimon
Peach Tree Borer.description: Spray trunk and scaffold limbs. Spray interval: 21 days.

Peach Tree Borer.name: Altacor
Peach Tree Borer.description: Do not exceed 261 g/ac per season.

Peach Tree Borer.name: Altacor MaX
Peach Tree Borer.description: Do not exceed 131 g/ac per season.

Peach Tree Borer.name: Delegate
Peach Tree Borer.description: For suppression. Cover trunk and scaffold limbs thoroughly.
